title: Rose geranium room spray
subtitle: A floral room and linen spray with rose geranium essential oil
excerpt: A room spray made from distilled water, vodka as a dispersant, and rose geranium essential oil. Mist into the air, onto soft furnishings, or onto pillow cases. The vodka helps the essential oil disperse through the water more evenly. Fill into a 100 ml amber glass bottle with a spray top.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): rose geranium at room spray concentrations. Alcohol as a dispersant for essential oils in water-based sprays: standard formulation practice.

content.text: A room spray disperses fragrance into the air through a fine mist. Because essential oils do not dissolve in water, this recipe uses vodka as an

to carry the rose geranium essential oil into a more even suspension before the water is added. Shake the bottle before each use to redistribute the oil. The scent lasts in a room for 30 to 60 minutes after spraying.
For linen sprays applied to pillow cases or bedding, do a

on a hidden patch first. Then spray the visible parts. Rose geranium essential oil has a pregnancy caution in the first trimester. Use an unscented spray, or pick a different essential oil during early pregnancy.

items.name: Distilled water
items.prepNote: Distilled water. Tap water introduces minerals that can cloud the spray and shorten the shelf life.

items.name: Vodka
items.prepNote: Standard vodka (40% ABV). Mix the essential oil into the vodka first before adding the water.

items.name: Rose geranium essential oil
items.prepNote: 3% of spray weight. Pelargonium roseum. Mix into the vodka before adding the water.

content.text: Weigh the rose geranium essential oil and vodka into a small dish and stir to combine. Add the distilled water and stir briefly. Using the funnel, pour into the amber glass bottle and fit the spray top. Shake gently to mix. Label with the making date.

Shake the bottle. Hold 20 cm from the target and mist 2 to 3 sprays into the air or onto soft furnishings. For a linen spray, mist from 30 cm above a freshly made bed. Allow the spray to dry before using the surface.

Store in the amber glass bottle away from heat and direct light. Shake before each use. Keeps for 6 months. The scent will fade before the 6-month mark if the bottle is opened frequently. Discard if the spray develops any cloudiness that does not clear on shaking, or if an off smell develops.
